data.title: Auto-schedule session resume when usage quota resets
data.description:
## Context

When orchestration skills (sprint, debug-everything, fix-bug) hit usage quota thresholds and pause sub-agent dispatch (per epic dso-t7af), the session currently stops and the user must manually resume after the billing window resets. This creates unnecessary friction — the system knows when the quota will reset (the OAuth endpoint returns resets_at timestamps) and could automatically schedule a session resumption.

## Success Criteria

1. When check-usage.sh returns a paused verdict, it also outputs the earliest reset timestamp from the OAuth response
2. The orchestration skill, upon receiving a pause verdict, offers to schedule an automatic session resume at the reset time
3. Scheduled resume launches a new Claude Code session that picks up where the paused session left off, using ticket checkpoint notes for state recovery
4. A configurable session.auto_resume config key enables/disables auto-scheduling (default: prompt user)

## Dependencies

- Depends on dso-t7af (Remove 5-sub-agent cap): requires the check-usage.sh infrastructure and pause verdict mechanism

## Notes

Inspired by mironmax/claudecode-plugins scheduler-plugin pattern which auto-schedules session resumption at the usage reset time.
